raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft makes causal and comparative claims whose premise-to-conclusion flow would need checking as part of substantive improvement.","R1.3":"The reported ECE, benchmark, ablation, and distribution-shift claims must align with the tables and experimental results described in the text.","R1.5":"The excerpt mixes methods, results, related work, and interpretation, so section-level ordering and narrative arc are central to improvement.","R1.6":"The explicit request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, concision, register, and flow load-bearing.","R1.7":"The draft cites calibration literature and vague recent work, making attribution, citation coverage, and reference accuracy expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where supporting tables, appendix material, or external literature are unavailable in the artifact.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong claims such as meaningful under distribution shift, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not invent experiments, tables, or unsupported literature.","RX.4":"The projection includes avoiding changes that could mislead downstream readers about model reliability or empirical support.","RX.5":"Edits or recommendations would need to preserve traceability to the supplied artifact and its reported results."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes causal and comparative claims whose premise-to-conclusion flow would need checking as part of substantive improvement.
rationale.R1.3: The reported ECE, benchmark, ablation, and distribution-shift claims must align with the tables and experimental results described in the text.
rationale.R1.5: The excerpt mixes methods, results, related work, and interpretation, so section-level ordering and narrative arc are central to improvement.
rationale.R1.6: The explicit request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, concision, register, and flow load-bearing.
rationale.R1.7: The draft cites calibration literature and vague recent work, making attribution, citation coverage, and reference accuracy expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where supporting tables, appendix material, or external literature are unavailable in the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong claims such as meaningful under distribution shift, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not invent experiments, tables, or unsupported literature.
rationale.RX.4: The projection includes avoiding changes that could mislead downstream readers about model reliability or empirical support.
rationale.RX.5: Edits or recommendations would need to preserve traceability to the supplied artifact and its reported results.
